title: melica onoei subsp. scaberrima
intro: Melica onoei subsp. scaberrima, also known as scabrous melic grass, is a subspecies of the Melica onoei plant, a type of grass native to East Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce swelling and protect against cell damage. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems, and is believed to offer benefits such as immune system support, reduced inflammation, and improved cardiovascular health. In traditional herbal preparations, Melica onoei subsp. scaberrima is often used as a tea or decoction, or made into a powder for internal or external use.
